original: First use of a drone to deliver vaccines - to island of Erromango, Vanuatu, by Unicef
World's first undersea roundabout opens named 'the Jellyfish' as part of a 11.2 km (6.9 miles) tunnel network for the Faroe Islands with illuminated artwork by Tróndur Patursson
NASA unveils plans for lunar colony & manned missions to Mars
"The Fellowship of the Ring", 1stLord of the Ringsfilm is released. Makes US$47 million US opening weekend, $871 million total worldwide.

"Miracle at the New Meadowlands", Philadelphia Eagles trail New York Giants by 21 points with eight minutes to play, before scoring 4 touchdowns in final 7 minutes, including dramatic walk-off punt returned for a touchdown by DeSean Jackson
World's largest indoor water park Tropical Islands Resort, opens in the Aerium, an old airship hanger, in the world's largest free-standing hall, south of Berlin, Germany
Apollo 17 (last of Apollo Moon landing series) returns to Earth, splashing down in the Pacific Ocean, southeast of Samoa
Earliest fossilized trees, 386 million years old, found at a quarry in Cairo, New York, study published in "Current Biology"
The Lakotah people, a Native American tribe, proclaim independence and withdraw all their treaties with the United States. Establish the Republic of Lakotah, as a separate country.
Dawson City hockey team begins 9 day walk to get a boat to Seattle to catch a train to Ottawa to play in Stanley Cup on Jan 13 1905
Robert Ripley begins his "Believe It or Not" column (NY Globe)
Robinson Crusoe,Daniel Defoe's fictional character, leaves his island after 28 years of being marooned (according to the famous novel)
"A Christmas Carol" byCharles Dickensis published, 6,000 copies sold
Oakland judge exonerates Glynn Simmons, 70, after he spent 48 years in jail for a murder he didn't commit - longest known wrongful sentence in the US
Wayne Gretzky, 23, is 18th & youngest NHL-er to score 1,000 points
The original FIFA World Cup trophy, the Jules Rimet Trophy, is stolen from the headquarters of the Brazilian Football Confederation in Rio de Janeiro
Alex Puccioascends Terre De Sienne V13 (8B) at Hueco Tanks, six months after spinal surgery
190 countries agree to protect 30% of world's land and oceans by 2030 and other measures to halt declining global biodiversity at UN Biodiversity Conference in Montreal
HMS Beagle withCharles Darwinaboard arrives in New Zealand

After 31 days at sea in a small boat, William Halford and 3 others reach the island of Kauai, Hawaii to seek help for the shipwrecked USS Saginaw. A capsize in the breakers meant only Halford survived.
33 unknown Bach keyboard works found in Yale library
First radio broadcast from space, US PresidentDwight D. Eisenhowersends a Christmas message "to all mankind, America's wish for peace on Earth and goodwill to men everywhere"
Australian state of New South Wales announces 7-day state of emergency amid extreme heat and over 100 bushfires that have burnt for two months
Mary Lund is 1st woman to receive a Jarvik VII artificial heart at Abbott Northwestern Hospital, Minneapolis
Mitchell Feigenbaum, American mathematical physicist who pioneered chaos theory, born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 2019)[1]
Criss Angel [Christopher Nicholas Sarantakos], American magician and illusionist (Criss Angel Mindfreak), born in Hempstead, New York

Houston Rockets set NBA single-game record with 26 three-pointers in 136-118 victory over Washington Wizards; 8 players hit from deep as Rockets shoot 26-of-55 (47.3%); James Harden leads charge, shooting 6-of-11 on 3s
NHL Boston Bruins Ken Linseman & St. Louis Blues Doug Gilmore set a record for the fastest two goals in league history, scoring 2 seconds apart
Comedy film "9 to 5" starringDolly Parton,Jane FondaandLily Tomlinis released

Robert Sherman, American Grammy and Academy Award-winning songwriter, with his brother, Richard (Mary Poppins; The Jungle Book; Chitty Chitty Bang Bang; "You're Sixteen"), born in New York City (d. 2012)[1]
Jack Johnsonfights fellow African-American Jim Johnson to a draw in 10 rounds for the vacant world heavyweight boxing title in Paris, 1st time two black fighters compete for the title
Theresa Vaughn, 24, confesses in court in Sheffield, England, to being married 61 times over 5 years in 50 cities in three countries
Piper Cherokee plane crashes into Baltimore Memorial Stadium upper stands, 10 minutes after Colts lose 40-14 to Pittsburgh Steelers, no one seriously hurt
Benjamin Franklin, under the pseudonym Richard Saunders, begins publication of "Poor Richard's Almanack"; he would produce an annual issue for the next 25 years
William J.L. Sladen, Welsh-American ecologist and Antarctic explorer whose work led to a ban on pesticide DDT, born in Newport, Wales (d. 2017)[1]
Transit 5A1, 1st operational navigational satellite, launched
Maurice White, American funk-rock singer-songwriter (Earth, Wind & Fire - "September"), born in Memphis, Tennessee (d. 2016)
Mileva Marić, Serbian physicist, mathematician, and first wife of Albert Einstein (thought to have contributed to Albert's early work), born in Titel, Kingdom of Hungary (d. 1948)
Richard Leakey, Kenyan paleoanthropologist, conservationist and politician, born in Nairobi, Kenya
USSR frees dissidentAndrei Sakharovfrom internal exile
A record high barometric pressure of 1085.6 hPa (32.06 inHg) is recorded at Tosontsengel, Khövsgöl Province, Mongolia.
Cyril Dean Darlington, English biologist and geneticist who discovered the mechanics of chromosomal crossover and its role in inheritance and evolution, born in Chorley, England (d. 1981)
William DeVries, American cardiothoracic surgeon (artificial heart), born in Brooklyn, New York
CBS airs "Homecoming A Christmas Story" as a TV movie that evolves into long running series "The Waltons"
Carl Perkinsrecords "Blue Suede Shoes" for Sun Records at Memphis Recording Service studio
Former Welsh and Lions captain Gareth Thomas is the first international rugby player to reveal he is gay
Drones flying over Gatwick airport, England, causes delays and cancellations for 800 flights and 110,000 people

Paul McCartneywraps up his 'Got Back' tour at London's O2 Arena, with guest appearances from Ron Wood during "Get Back", andRingo Starrdrumming on "Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band" and "Helter Skelter"
Larry Bird(Celtics) begins NBA free throw streak of 71 games

Arvydas Sabonis, Lithuanian Basketball HOF center (Olympic gold USSR 1988; bronze Lithuania 1992, 96; 6 × Euroscar Player of the Year; Mr. Europa Player of the Year 1985, 97; Portland Trailblazers), born in Kaunas, Lithuania
Mulatu Astatke, Ethiopian jazz vibraphonist, percussionist, composer, and arranger, born in Jimma, Ethiopian Empire
Washington settles his troops at Valley Forge, Pennsylvania for the winter
William Pitt the Youngerbecomes the youngest ever British Prime Minister at age 24
Mercury-Redstone 1A reaches 210 km in test flight
John Lever takes 7-46 in 1st Test Cricket innings, v India Delhi
Oklahoma's College football team gets 3 year probation
Professor Longhair [Henry Roeland Byrd], American boogie-woogie, New Orleans R&B, and mambo pianist and singer ("Tipitina"; "Go To The Mardis Gras"), born in Bogalusa, Louisiana (d. 1980)
Mike Lookinland, American actor (Bobby-Brady Bunch), born in Mt Pleasant, Utah
Jill Talley, American actress (SpongeBob SquarePants), born in Chicago, Illinois
"Mean" Gene Okerlund, American pro wrestling interviewer, announcer and television host (WWF, WCW), born in Brookings, South Dakota (d. 2019)
